ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general curry rice dish class; no eggplant-specific curry rice entry exists. FOODON_00002809 used as closest defensible parent class.
ontology.foodex2_description: Rice-based dish with vegetables and curry sauce
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a dedicated eggplant curry rice code; A0FJP (rice-based dish) used as closest defensible anchor. Eggplant and curry sauce are qualifying facets.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code exists for eggplant curry rice as a specific food concept.

allergens: wheat (curry roux typically contains wheat flour)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard plate of eggplant curry rice as served in a Japanese restaurant or canteen in Singapore is approximately 350 g, comprising roughly 200 g steamed white rice and 150 g eggplant curry sauce.

nutrient_source.title: Eggplant Curry Rice — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Input record carries HPB provenance at confidence 0.95 for all per_100g fields. Values are preserved as supplied by the HPB Foodo database. Saturated fat is not reported by HPB for this entry and is estimated from USDA FoodData Central analogous Japanese curry rice dish (USDA SR Legacy #21340 and component analysis); see notes.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for eggplant curry rice in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI estimated at 65 based on the GI of white rice (GI ~72, University of Sydney GI Database) moderated downward by the presence of dietary fibre (3.7 g/100g from eggplant and vegetables) and the mixed-dish effect of curry sauce, consistent with published mixed-meal GI attenuation principles (Brand-Miller et al., Diabetes Care 2008). Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (65 × 25.19 × 3.5) / 100 ≈ 30.5 for a 350 g serving (88.2 g carbohydrate).

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 231 mg/100g; partially offset by potassium 327 mg/100g (Na:K ratio < 1)
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension should limit portion size to 200–250 g to keep sodium intake below 580 mg per serving; request reduced-sodium curry roux and minimal added oil, as eggplant absorbs cooking oil readily.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Carbohydrate 25.19 g/100g from white rice base (high-GI component); dietary fibre 3.7 g/100g partially attenuates postprandial glucose rise
type2_diabetes.guidance: Patients with type 2 diabetes should limit serving to 200–250 g and consider substituting brown rice to lower the glycemic response; the 3.7 g/100g dietary fibre from eggplant and vegetables provides meaningful glucose-moderating benefit, but portion control remains essential given the estimated GI of 65.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate fat 4.21 g/100g with estimated saturated fat ~1.2 g/100g; sodium 231 mg/100g; cholesterol confirmed 0 mg/100g
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease may consume this dish in moderate portions (200–250 g); the absence of cholesterol and relatively low saturated fat are favourable, but sodium content and oil absorption by eggplant during cooking warrant requesting minimal-oil preparation and reduced-sodium curry roux.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Eggplant is a nightshade vegetable; solanine content may exacerbate joint inflammation in RA-sensitive individuals
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis who are sensitive to nightshade vegetables should avoid this dish or substitute eggplant with non-nightshade vegetables such as zucchini or mushroom; curry spices including turmeric and ginger provide anti-inflammatory benefit but do not fully offset the nightshade concern.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Eggplant Curry Rice (ナスカレーライス, Nasu Karē Raisu) is a Japanese-style curry rice dish featuring stewed eggplant (aubergine/brinjal) in a thick, mildly spiced Japanese curry sauce served over a bed of steamed white rice. Japanese curry is distinctively milder, sweeter, and thicker than South Asian curries, typically prepared using a roux-based curry block containing wheat flour, curry spices, and sometimes apple or honey for sweetness. The eggplant absorbs the curry sauce during simmering, yielding a soft, silky texture with a rich umami flavour. This dish is widely available in Japanese restaurants and food courts in Singapore. Nutritionally, per 100 g it provides 164 kcal, 6.5 g protein, 4.21 g fat, 25.19 g carbohydrate, 3.7 g dietary fibre, and 231 mg sodium. The high dietary fibre from eggplant moderates postprandial glucose absorption. Potassium at 327 mg/100g partially offsets the sodium load. Cholesterol is 0 mg/100g. The estimated glycemic index is 65 (medium), moderated from the white rice base by the fibre and mixed-dish effect. Clinically, portion control is recommended for patients with type 2 diabetes, hypertension, and cardiovascular disease. Patients with rheumatoid arthritis should note that eggplant is a nightshade vegetable and may exacerbate joint inflammation in sensitive individuals.

confidence_reason: Core macronutrient and micronutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database at confidence 0.95. Saturated fat estimated from USDA FoodData Central analogous data and documented. GI estimated from University of Sydney GI Database white rice reference with documented mixed-dish attenuation rationale.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (1.2 g/100g) is estimated from USDA FoodData Central analogous Japanese curry rice dish data (SR Legacy) as HPB does not report saturated fat for this entry; this is an approximation and should be replaced with a direct HPB or laboratory value when available. 2. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions, vitamin panel, and moisture data for this specific eggplant curry rice preparation are not available in the HPB Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026-04-16. 3. Cholesterol is 0.0 mg/100g as confirmed by HPB source; this is a plant-based dish with no animal-derived ingredients contributing cholesterol. 4. GI of 65 is an analogous estimate based on white rice GI (University of Sydney GI Database) moderated by dietary fibre and mixed-dish effect; a direct GI measurement for this dish is not available in any approved GI source. 5. Dietary flags: is_vegan set to false because commercial Japanese curry roux blocks typically contain dairy (butter/milk solids); is_halal set to false as standard Japanese curry roux may contain pork-derived ingredients; consumers should verify product labels. 6. FoodOn URI FOODON_00002809 and FoodEx2 code A0FJP are approximate matches; no exact ontology code exists for eggplant curry rice.
